title: Spicy Hangover Aussie Pork Belly Bao
description: A flavorful and spicy breakfast bao featuring tender pork belly slices in a gluten-free friendly filling, designed to cure even the worst hangovers.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine pork belly, gochujang, soy sauce, brown sugar, garlic, and ginger. Mix well to coat.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add vegetable oil and cook the pork belly mixture until caramelized and sticky, about 10 minutes.
While the pork is cooking, prepare the bao dough according to the package instructions.
Divide the dough into small balls, about 1-inch diameter.
Roll each ball into a thin circle, about 3 inches in diameter.
Place a tablespoon of the pork belly mixture onto the center of each circle.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half-moon shape, and press the edges to seal.
Heat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium heat.
Cook the bao for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until golden brown.
Serve the bao hot, garnished with green chili slices, cilantro, and scallions.
